output:
CVE-2022-3614 describes a vulnerability in Octopus Deploy where users of certain browsers using Active Directory (AD) to sign-in to Octopus Server could bypass authentication checks. The flaw involves improper validation of redirect URLs, allowing users to be redirected without validation, leading to potential phishing risks. This type of vulnerability is categorized under CWE-601, which deals with open redirect issues.

The vulnerability has a severity level of MEDIUM. Exploiting this security flaw can facilitate phishing attacks by enabling attackers to redirect users to malicious sites without users being aware of the change. The vulnerability affects multiple versions of Octopus Server from version 3.5 up to 2022.3.10750 and from 2022.4 up to 2022.4.8063.

Mitigation techniques include implementing thorough validation of redirect URLs and applying security controls such as those suggested in techniques M1048, M1037, M1030, M1016, M1026, M1050, M1035, and M1051. Awareness and user education can also play a significant role in mitigating the risk of phishing attacks associated with this vulnerability. The associated MITRE ATT&CK technique is T1190, concerning vulnerabilities exploited in web applications.
